h2h_summary: Mexico has historically dominated Ecuador in the available recorded matchups, winning 5 times and drawing 1, with no wins for Ecuador in the visible records.
key_players_away: Willian Pacho (centre-back, UCL winner with PSG)
team_style_home: Mexico under Javier Aguirre deploy a 4-3-3 emphasizing defensive discipline, rapid counter-attacks, a quick passing game through midfield, and dangerous set pieces with strong aerial threats.
team_style_away: Ecuador under Beccacece deploy a compact 4-4-2 / 4-2-3-1 built on elite defensive organisation — conceding only 5 goals across 18 qualifying matches — and rely on rapid, disciplined counter-attacks rather than sustained possession.
wc_history_home: Mexico has appeared in 18 World Cups and reached the quarter-finals in 1970 and 1986 (both as host), but have been eliminated in the Round of 16 in seven consecutive tournaments from 1994 to 2022, a streak locals call 'El Quinto Partido' curse.
wc_history_away: Ecuador have qualified for four World Cups (2002, 2006, 2014, 2022), with their best finish being the Round of 16 in 2006, and memorably opened the 2022 tournament with a 2-0 win over hosts Qatar.
group_context: Round of 32 knockout — no draws; level after 90 goes to extra time and penalties. Winner advances to the Round of 16.
strengths_home: Home advantage at altitude in Estadio Azteca with massive crowd support
Experienced squad led by Raul Jimenez and veteran goalkeeper Ochoa, with seven World Cup appearances between key players
Rapid counter-attacking and set-piece danger with aerial threats from Cesar Montes and Johan Vasquez
strengths_away: Elite defensive solidity — only 5 goals conceded in 18 qualifying matches
World-class midfield pivot in Moises Caicedo providing interceptions, press resistance and ball-carrying
Dangerous and well-drilled counter-attacking with pace on the wings
